name: Chestnut Stuffing
recipeIngredient: 6 cups Day-old homemade-style white bread; tear pieces
3 tablespoons Fresh sage leaves; mince, or
2 tablespoons Fresh thyme leaves; mince, or
1 pound Fresh chestnuts; shell, peel, chop coarsely, or
¾ pound Vacuum-packed whole chestnuts; coarse chop
½ cup Parsley leaves; chop fine
recipeInstructions: Preheat the oven to 325°F. In a shallow baking pan arrange the bread pieces in one layer and bake them in the oven, stirring occasionally, for 10 to 15 minutes, or until they are golden brown. Transfer the bread to a large bowl. In a large skillet cook the onions, celery, sage, thyme, rosemary, and savory in butter over moderately low heat, stirring, until the vegetables are softened. Add the chestnuts and cook the mixture, stirring, for 1 minute. Add the vegetable mixture to the bread pieces, tossing well. Stir in the parsley and season with salt and pepper to taste. Let the stuffing cool completely. This stuffing may be made up to one day in advance and kept covered and chilled. Do not stuff the turkey cavities in advance. Enough to stuff a 12- to 14-pound turkey with extra to bake on the side.
